winner_selection_metric: minimum abs 95% all-topology coverage error plus 0.01 times interval width among non-control methods

control_projection_ledger.note: global train-run median; removes pair, topology, and B2 labels
train-run median by B2-containing vs downstream-only topology
available as frozen S05h atom-support ledger, not row-level residual refit
train-run median by sample/run family; held-out run excluded

finding: Topology-specific conformal calibration provides near-nominal interval coverage for frozen S05h/S05f residual methods, but B2-containing, saturation, baseline, and pile-up/anomaly support atoms remain the covariance-systematic driver.

next_tickets.title: S05n row-level atom-conditional projection coverage
next_tickets.body: Retain row-level support-atom labels in the frozen S05 residual export, then calibrate 68/95% two-ended projection intervals directly by pair, B2 topology, amplitude, saturation depth, q-template shift, baseline/lowering, pile-up/anomaly proxy, and run family. Compare the S05m winner against traditional S05d priors plus ridge, gradient-boosted trees, MLP, 1D-CNN, and support-gated CNN with run-block bootstrap CIs. This tests whether the S05m aggregate support-frontier caveat hides atom-level undercoverage.
